div.p: Each allocated string has exactly ONE owner at any time. Ownership transfers explicitly via assignment + free of old.
The function that calls malloc/strdup initially owns the memory. It must either free it or transfer ownership.
If expansion fails mid-way, ensure all intermediate allocations are freed in error path.
const char* word
char* working = strdup(word) char* expanded = expand_tilde(working) free(working) working = expanded
char* param_expanded = expand_parameter(working, ctx) free(working) working = param_expanded
char* cmd_expanded = expand_command_substitution(working, ctx) free(working) working = cmd_expanded

ul.li: Freed: After content copied to result
Note: fields[] point INTO original string

div.p: fields[0] ──► "word1\0word2\0word3" fields[1] ────────▲ fields[2] ─────────────▲ fields[3] = NULL
result.words[0] ──► "file1.txt" (strdup) result.words[1] ──► "file2.txt" (strdup) result.words[2] ──► "file3.txt" (strdup) result.words[3] = NULL

ul.li: Each match is strdup'd from readdir
